title: Multi-Sourced, Multi-Agent Evidence Retrieval for Fact-Checking
summary: Proposes WKGFC, a fact-checking system that uses knowledge graphs and LLM agents to retrieve structured evidence, addressing limitations of text-similarity-based retrieval that misses multi-hop semantic relations. The system employs an agentic Markov Decision Process to iteratively gather web evidence and verify claims against knowledge graphs, improving accuracy in detecting subtle factual correlations.
content:
arXiv:2603.00267v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Misinformation spreading over the Internet poses a significant threat to both societies and individuals, necessitating robust and scalable fact-checking that relies on retrieving accurate and trustworthy evidence. Previous methods rely on semantic and social-contextual patterns learned from training data, which limits their generalization to new data distributions. Recently, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) based methods have been proposed to utilize the reasoning capability of LLMs with retrieved grounding evidence documents. However, these methods largely rely on textual similarity for evidence retrieval and struggle to retrieve evidence that captures multi-hop semantic relations within rich document contents. These limitations lead to overlooking subtle factual correlations between the evidence and the claims to be fact-checked during evidence retrieval, thus causing inaccurate veracity predictions.
  To address these issues, we propose WKGFC, which exploits authorized open knowledge graph as a core resource of evidence. LLM-enabled retrieval is designed to assess the claims and retrieve the most relevant knowledge subgraphs, forming structured evidence for fact verification. To augment the knowledge graph evidence, we retrieve web contents for completion. The above process is implemented as an automatic Markov Decision Process (MDP): A reasoning LLM agent decides what actions to take according to the current evidence and the claims. To adapt the MDP for fact-checking, we use prompt optimization to fine-tune the agentic LLM.

zh.title: 多源多代理证据检索用于事实核查
zh.summary: 提出WKGFC，一个事实核查系统，使用知识图和LLM代理来检索结构化证据，解决了基于文本相似性的检索在缺失多跳语义关系上的局限性。该系统采用代理性马尔可夫决策过程来迭代地收集网络证据并根据知识图验证声明，提高了检测微妙事实相关性的准确性。

fr.title: Récupération d'évidences multi-sources et multi-agents pour la vérification des faits
fr.summary: Propose WKGFC, un système de vérification des faits qui utilise des graphes de connaissances et des agents LLM pour récupérer des preuves structurées, abordant les limitations de la récupération basée sur la similarité de texte qui manque les relations sémantiques multi-sauts. Le système emploie un processus de décision de Markov agentique pour recueillir itérativement les preuves du web et vérifier les affirmations par rapport aux graphes de connaissances, améliorant la précision dans la détection des corrélations factuelles subtiles.

de.title: Multi-Source-, Multi-Agent-Evidenzbeschaffung zur Faktenprüfung
de.summary: Schlägt WKGFC vor, ein Faktenprüfungssystem, das Wissensgraphen und LLM-Agenten nutzt, um strukturierte Beweise abzurufen und dabei die Grenzen des textähnlichkeitsbasierten Abrufs zu überwinden, der Multi-Hop-Semantikrelationen übersieht. Das System nutzt einen agentialen Markov-Entscheidungsprozess, um iterativ Web-Beweise zu sammeln und Behauptungen gegen Wissensgraphen zu überprüfen, was die Genauigkeit beim Erkennen subtiler faktischer Korrelationen verbessert.

es.title: Recuperación de evidencia multi-fuente y multi-agente para verificación de hechos
es.summary: Propone WKGFC, un sistema de verificación de hechos que utiliza gráficos de conocimiento y agentes LLM para recuperar evidencia estructurada, abordando las limitaciones de la recuperación basada en similitud de texto que pierden relaciones semánticas multi-salto. El sistema emplea un proceso de decisión de Markov agentivo para recopilar iterativamente evidencia web y verificar afirmaciones contra gráficos de conocimiento, mejorando la precisión en la detección de correlaciones factuales sutiles.

title: TraderBench: How Robust Are AI Agents in Adversarial Capital Markets?
summary: Introduces TraderBench, a benchmark combining expert-verified tasks with adversarial trading simulations to evaluate AI agents in finance without judge variance. Testing 13 models reveals that most achieve only fixed non-adaptive strategies with minimal variation across market conditions, indicating a critical gap in genuine market adaptation capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.00285v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Evaluating AI agents in finance faces two key challenges: static benchmarks require costly expert annotation yet miss the dynamic decision-making central to real-world trading, while LLM-based judges introduce uncontrolled variance on domain-specific tasks. We introduce TraderBench, a benchmark that addresses both issues. It combines expert-verified static tasks (knowledge retrieval, analytical reasoning) with adversarial trading simulations scored purely on realized performance-Sharpe ratio, returns, and drawdown-eliminating judge variance entirely. The framework features two novel tracks: crypto trading with four progressive market-manipulation transforms, and options derivatives scoring across P&L accuracy, Greeks, and risk management. Trading scenarios can be refreshed with new market data to prevent benchmark contamination. Evaluating 13 models (8B open-source to frontier) on ~50 tasks, we find: (1) 8 of 13 models score ~33 on crypto with <1-point variation across adversarial conditions, exposing fixed non-adaptive strategies; (2) extended thinking helps retrieval (+26 points) but has zero impact on trading (+0.3 crypto, -0.1 options). These findings reveal that current agents lack genuine market adaptation, underscoring the need for performance-grounded evaluation in finance.

zh.title: TraderBench：AI代理在对抗性资本市场中的鲁棒性如何？
zh.summary: 介绍TraderBench，一个结合专家验证任务和对抗交易模拟的基准，用于评估金融领域中的AI代理而不存在评判差异。对13个模型的测试表明，大多数只能实现固定的非自适应策略，在市场条件中的变化极小，表明在真正的市场适应能力方面存在关键缺陷。

fr.title: TraderBench : Quelle est la robustesse des agents IA dans les marchés des capitaux adverses?
fr.summary: Présente TraderBench, un benchmark combinant des tâches vérifiées par des experts avec des simulations de trading adverses pour évaluer les agents IA dans la finance sans variance de jugement. Les tests sur 13 modèles révèlent que la plupart ne réalisent que des stratégies fixes non adaptatives avec une variation minimale selon les conditions du marché, indiquant un écart critique dans les capacités d'adaptation réelles au marché.

de.title: TraderBench: Wie robust sind KI-Agenten auf adversarialen Kapitalmärkten?
de.summary: Stellt TraderBench vor, einen Benchmark, der von Experten überprüfte Aufgaben mit gegnerischen Handelssimulationen kombiniert, um KI-Agenten in der Finanzwirtschaft ohne Urteilsvarianz zu bewerten. Tests mit 13 Modellen zeigen, dass die meisten nur feste, nicht-adaptive Strategien mit minimalen Schwankungen über Marktbedingungen hinweg erreichen, was auf eine kritische Lücke in echten Marktanpassungsfähigkeiten hindeutet.

es.title: TraderBench: ¿Cuán robustos son los agentes de IA en mercados de capital adversariales?
es.summary: Presenta TraderBench, un punto de referencia que combina tareas verificadas por expertos con simulaciones de trading adversarial para evaluar agentes de IA en finanzas sin varianza de juez. Las pruebas en 13 modelos revelan que la mayoría logran solo estrategias fijas no adaptativas con variación mínima en las condiciones del mercado, indicando una brecha crítica en las capacidades reales de adaptación al mercado.

title: DIG to Heal: Scaling General-purpose Agent Collaboration via Explainable Dynamic Decision Paths
summary: Introduces the Dynamic Interaction Graph (DIG) to visualize and explain how autonomous LLM agents collaborate without predefined roles, making emergent collaboration observable for the first time. By capturing agent activations as a time-evolving causal network, DIG enables real-time identification and correction of collaboration failures that are otherwise difficult to interpret.
content:
arXiv:2603.00309v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The increasingly popular agentic AI paradigm promises to harness the power of multiple, general-purpose large language model (LLM) agents to collaboratively complete complex tasks. While many agentic AI systems utilize predefined workflows or agent roles in order to reduce complexity, ideally these agents would be truly autonomous, able to achieve emergent collaboration even as the number of collaborating agents increases. Yet in practice, such unstructured interactions can lead to redundant work and cascading failures that are difficult to interpret or correct. In this work, we study multi-agent systems composed of general-purpose LLM agents that operate without predefined roles, control flow, or communication constraints, relying instead on emergent collaboration to solve problems. We introduce the Dynamic Interaction Graph (DIG), which captures emergent collaboration as a time-evolving causal network of agent activations and interactions. DIG makes emergent collaboration observable and explainable for the first time, enabling real-time identification, explanation, and correction of collaboration-induced error patterns directly from agents' collaboration paths. Thus, DIG fills a critical gap in understanding how general LLM agents solve problems together in truly agentic multi-agent systems. The project webpage can be found at: https://happyeureka.github.io/dig.

zh.title: DIG to Heal：通过可解释的动态决策路径扩展通用代理协作
zh.summary: 介绍动态交互图(DIG)来可视化和解释自主LLM代理如何在没有预定义角色的情况下协作，首次使新兴协作可观察。通过将代理激活捕获为时间演化的因果网络，DIG能够实时识别和纠正否则难以解释的协作失败。

fr.title: DIG to Heal : Mise à l'échelle de la collaboration d'agents polyvalents via des chemins de décision dynamiques explicables
fr.summary: Présente le Graphique d'interaction dynamique (DIG) pour visualiser et expliquer comment les agents LLM autonomes collaborent sans rôles prédéfinis, rendant la collaboration émergente observable pour la première fois. En capturant les activations d'agents comme un réseau causal évolutif dans le temps, DIG permet l'identification en temps réel et la correction des défaillances de collaboration qui sont autrement difficiles à interpréter.

de.title: DIG to Heal: Skalierung der Zusammenarbeit von Mehrzweck-Agenten durch erklärbare dynamische Entscheidungspfade
de.summary: Stellt den Dynamic Interaction Graph (DIG) vor, um zu visualisieren und zu erklären, wie autonome LLM-Agenten ohne vordefinierte Rollen zusammenarbeiten und emergente Zusammenarbeit erstmals beobachtbar machen. Durch die Erfassung von Agentaktivierungen als zeitlich evolvierende kausale Netzwerk ermöglicht DIG die Echtzeitidentifikation und Korrektur von Zusammenarbeitsfehlen, die sonst schwer zu interpretieren sind.

es.title: DIG to Heal: Escalado de la colaboración de agentes de propósito general a través de caminos de decisión dinámicos explicables
es.summary: Presenta el Gráfico de Interacción Dinámica (DIG) para visualizar y explicar cómo los agentes LLM autónomos colaboran sin roles predefinidos, haciendo que la colaboración emergente sea observable por primera vez. Al capturar las activaciones de agentes como una red causal que evoluciona en el tiempo, DIG permite la identificación en tiempo real y la corrección de fallas de colaboración que de otro modo serían difíciles de interpretar.

title: How Well Do Multimodal Models Reason on ECG Signals?
summary: Proposes a dual-component framework for evaluating how multimodal LLMs reason about ECG signals, decomposing analysis into perception (identifying signal patterns) and deduction (applying clinical logic). The approach uses code generation to verify temporal structures and retrieval-based comparison against established clinical criteria, enabling scalable assessment of reasoning validity in healthcare applications.
content:
arXiv:2603.00312v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While multimodal large language models offer a promising solution to the "black box" nature of health AI by generating interpretable reasoning traces, verifying the validity of these traces remains a critical challenge. Existing evaluation methods are either unscalable, relying on manual clinician review, or superficial, utilizing proxy metrics (e.g. QA) that fail to capture the semantic correctness of clinical logic. In this work, we introduce a reproducible framework for evaluating reasoning in ECG signals. We propose decomposing reasoning into two distinct, components: (i) Perception, the accurate identification of patterns within the raw signal, and (ii) Deduction, the logical application of domain knowledge to those patterns. To evaluate Perception, we employ an agentic framework that generates code to empirically verify the temporal structures described in the reasoning trace. To evaluate Deduction, we measure the alignment of the model's logic against a structured database of established clinical criteria in a retrieval-based approach. This dual-verification method enables the scalable assessment of "true" reasoning capabilities.

zh.title: 多模态模型在心电图信号推理中的表现如何?
zh.summary: 提出了一个双层框架,用于评估多模态大语言模型如何推理心电图信号,将分析分解为感知(识别信号模式)和推理(应用临床逻辑)。该方法使用代码生成来验证时间结构,并通过基于检索的比较与已建立的临床标准进行对比,能够对医疗应用中的推理有效性进行可扩展的评估。

fr.title: Comment les modèles multimodaux raisonnent-ils sur les signaux ECG ?
fr.summary: Propose un cadre à deux composants pour évaluer comment les LLM multimodaux raisonnent sur les signaux ECG, en décomposant l'analyse en perception (identification des motifs de signal) et déduction (application de la logique clinique). L'approche utilise la génération de code pour vérifier les structures temporelles et la comparaison basée sur la récupération par rapport aux critères cliniques établis, permettant une évaluation évolutive de la validité du raisonnement dans les applications de soins de santé.

de.title: Wie gut argumentieren multimodale Modelle bei EKG-Signalen?
de.summary: Schlägt ein zweikomponentiges Framework vor, um zu bewerten, wie multimodale LLMs über EKG-Signale argumentieren, indem die Analyse in Wahrnehmung (Identifizierung von Signalmustern) und Deduktion (Anwendung von klinischer Logik) zerlegt wird. Der Ansatz nutzt Code-Generierung, um zeitliche Strukturen zu überprüfen und abrufbasierte Vergleiche mit etablierten klinischen Kriterien durchzuführen, wodurch eine skalierbare Bewertung der Gültigkeit von Argumentationen in Gesundheitsanwendungen ermöglicht wird.

es.title: ¿Qué tan bien razonan los modelos multimodales sobre señales de ECG?
es.summary: Propone un marco de dos componentes para evaluar cómo los LLM multimodales razonan sobre señales de ECG, descomponiendo el análisis en percepción (identificación de patrones de señal) y deducción (aplicación de lógica clínica). El enfoque utiliza generación de código para verificar estructuras temporales y comparación basada en recuperación contra criterios clínicos establecidos, permitiendo una evaluación escalable de la validez del razonamiento en aplicaciones de atención médica.

title: EmCoop: A Framework and Benchmark for Embodied Cooperation Among LLM Agents
summary: Introduces EmCoop, a benchmark for studying how multiple embodied LLM agents collaborate in dynamic environments where tasks exceed single-agent capabilities. The framework separates cognitive reasoning from embodied interaction layers and proposes process-level metrics to diagnose collaboration quality and failure modes beyond final task success.
content:
arXiv:2603.00349v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Real-world scenarios increasingly require multiple embodied agents to collaborate in dynamic environments under embodied constraints, as many tasks exceed the capabilities of any single agent. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable high-level cognitive coordination through reasoning, planning, and natural language communication. However, fine-grained analyses of how such collaboration emerges, unfolds, and contributes to task success in embodied multi-agent systems are difficult to conduct with existing benchmarks. In this paper, we introduce EmCoop, a benchmark framework for studying cooperation in LLM-based embodied multi-agent systems. Our framework separates a high-level cognitive layer from a low-level embodied interaction layer, allowing us to characterize agent cooperation through their interleaved dynamics over time. Given a cooperation-constrained embodied task, we propose generalizable, process-level metrics that diagnose collaboration quality and failure modes, beyond final task success. We instantiate our framework in two embodied environments that scale to arbitrary numbers of agents and support diverse communication topologies, and use these instantiations to demonstrate how EmCoop enables systematic analysis of cooperation dynamics across team sizes and task settings. The project web page can be found at: https://happyeureka.github.io/emcoop.

zh.title: EmCoop:具身LLM代理间协作的框架和基准
zh.summary: 介绍了EmCoop,这是一个用于研究多个具身LLM代理如何在动态环境中协作的基准,其中任务超过单个代理的能力。该框架将认知推理与具身交互层分离,并提出了流程级指标来诊断协作质量和超越最终任务成功的失败模式。

fr.title: EmCoop : Un cadre et un repère pour la coopération incarnée entre les agents LLM
fr.summary: Introduit EmCoop, un repère pour étudier comment plusieurs agents LLM incarnés collaborent dans des environnements dynamiques où les tâches dépassent les capacités d'un seul agent. Le cadre sépare le raisonnement cognitif des couches d'interaction incarnée et propose des métriques au niveau des processus pour diagnostiquer la qualité de la collaboration et les modes de défaillance au-delà du simple succès de la tâche.

de.title: EmCoop: Ein Framework und Benchmark für verkörperte Zusammenarbeit zwischen LLM-Agenten
de.summary: Stellt EmCoop vor, einen Benchmark zur Untersuchung der Zusammenarbeit mehrerer verkörperter LLM-Agenten in dynamischen Umgebungen, in denen Aufgaben die Fähigkeiten einzelner Agenten übersteigen. Das Framework trennt kognitives Denken von verkörperten Interaktionsebenen und schlägt Metriken auf Prozessebene vor, um die Zusammenarbeitqualität und Ausfallmodi über den bloßen Aufgabenerfolg hinaus zu diagnostizieren.

es.title: EmCoop: Un marco y referencia para la cooperación encarnada entre agentes LLM
es.summary: Introduce EmCoop, un referencia para estudiar cómo múltiples agentes LLM encarnados colaboran en entornos dinámicos donde las tareas superan las capacidades de un solo agente. El marco separa el razonamiento cognitivo de las capas de interacción encarnada y propone métricas a nivel de proceso para diagnosticar la calidad de la colaboración y los modos de falla más allá del simple éxito de la tarea.

title: Monotropic Artificial Intelligence: Toward a Cognitive Taxonomy of Domain-Specialized Language Models
summary: Challenges the assumption that larger, generalist models represent progress, proposing instead "Monotropic AI" that deliberately sacrifices breadth for extraordinary depth within narrow domains. Demonstrates viability through Mini-Enedina, a 37.5M-parameter model excelling at beam analysis, reframing AI research to value specialization alongside general intelligence pursuits.
content:
arXiv:2603.00350v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The prevailing paradigm in artificial intelligence research equates progress with scale: larger models trained on broader datasets are presumed to yield superior capabilities. This assumption, while empirically productive for general-purpose applications, obscures a fundamental epistemological tension between breadth and depth of knowledge. We introduce the concept of \emph{Monotropic Artificial Intelligence} -- language models that deliberately sacrifice generality to achieve extraordinary precision within narrowly circumscribed domains. Drawing on the cognitive theory of monotropism developed to understand autistic cognition, we argue that intense specialization represents not a limitation but an alternative cognitive architecture with distinct advantages for safety-critical applications. We formalize the defining characteristics of monotropic models, contrast them with conventional polytropic architectures, and demonstrate their viability through Mini-Enedina, a 37.5-million-parameter model that achieves near-perfect performance on Timoshenko beam analysis while remaining deliberately incompetent outside its domain. Our framework challenges the implicit assumption that artificial general intelligence constitutes the sole legitimate aspiration of AI research, proposing instead a cognitive ecology in which specialized and generalist systems coexist complementarily.

zh.title: 单向人工智能:走向领域专业化语言模型的认知分类学
zh.summary: 挑战了更大的通用模型代表进步的假设,反而提出了"单向人工智能",它故意牺牲广度来在狭窄的领域中获得非凡的深度。通过Mini-Enedina展示了可行性,这是一个3750万参数的模型,在梁分析中表现出色,将人工智能研究重新定向为重视专业化和通用智能追求。

fr.title: Intelligence Artificielle Monotrope : Vers une taxonomie cognitive des modèles de langage spécialisés en domaine
fr.summary: Remet en question l'hypothèse selon laquelle les modèles plus grands et généralistes représentent un progrès, en proposant plutôt l'« IA monotrope » qui sacrifie délibérément la largeur pour une profondeur extraordinaire dans des domaines étroits. Démontre la viabilité grâce à Mini-Enedina, un modèle de 37,5M de paramètres excellent à l'analyse de poutres, réorientant la recherche en IA pour valoriser la spécialisation aux côtés des poursuites d'intelligence générale.

de.title: Monotrope Künstliche Intelligenz: Hin zu einer kognitiven Taxonomie von domänenspezialisierten Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Hinterfragt die Annahme, dass größere, verallgemeinerte Modelle Fortschritt darstellen, und schlägt stattdessen "Monotrope KI" vor, die bewusst Breite für außergewöhnliche Tiefe in engen Domänen opfert. Demonstriert die Durchführbarkeit durch Mini-Enedina, ein 37,5-Millionen-Parameter-Modell, das in der Trägeranalyse hervorragende Leistungen zeigt, und ändert die KI-Forschung dahingehend, Spezialisierung neben allgemeinen Intelligenzzielen zu bewerten.

es.title: Inteligencia Artificial Monotrópica: Hacia una taxonomía cognitiva de modelos de lenguaje especializados en dominio
es.summary: Cuestiona la suposición de que los modelos más grandes y generalistas representan progreso, proponiendo en su lugar la "IA monotrópica" que sacrifica deliberadamente la amplitud por una profundidad extraordinaria dentro de dominios estrechos. Demuestra viabilidad a través de Mini-Enedina, un modelo de 37,5 millones de parámetros que destaca en análisis de vigas, reorientando la investigación en IA para valorar la especialización junto con la búsqueda de inteligencia general.

title: Conservative Equilibrium Discovery in Offline Game-Theoretic Multiagent Reinforcement Learning
summary: Extends Policy Space Response Oracles for offline game-solving by quantifying uncertainty in limited datasets and conservatively selecting strategies with higher probability of low regret. Introduces COffeE-PSRO, which combines conservatism principles with a novel meta-strategy solver designed for settings where verifying true equilibrium is infeasible.
content:
arXiv:2603.00374v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Offline learning of strategies takes data efficiency to its extreme by restricting algorithms to a fixed dataset of state-action trajectories. We consider the problem in a mixed-motive multiagent setting, where the goal is to solve a game under the offline learning constraint. We first frame this problem in terms of selecting among candidate equilibria. Since datasets may inform only a small fraction of game dynamics, it is generally infeasible in offline game-solving to even verify a proposed solution is a true equilibrium. Therefore, we consider the relative probability of low regret (i.e., closeness to equilibrium) across candidates based on the information available. Specifically, we extend Policy Space Response Oracles (PSRO), an online game-solving approach, by quantifying game dynamics uncertainty and modifying the RL objective to skew towards solutions more likely to have low regret in the true game. We further propose a novel meta-strategy solver, tailored for the offline setting, to guide strategy exploration in PSRO. Our incorporation of Conservatism principles from Offline reinforcement learning approaches for strategy Exploration gives our approach its name: COffeE-PSRO. Experiments demonstrate COffeE-PSRO's ability to extract lower-regret solutions than state-of-the-art offline approaches and reveal relationships between algorithmic components empirical game fidelity, and overall performance.

zh.title: 离线博弈论多智能体强化学习中的保守均衡发现
zh.summary: 扩展策略空间响应预言机用于离线博弈求解，通过量化有限数据集中的不确定性并保守地选择具有较低遗憾概率较高的策略。引入COffeE-PSRO，该方法结合保守性原则与新颖的元策略求解器，适用于无法验证真实均衡的设置。

fr.title: Découverte d'équilibre conservateur dans l'apprentissage par renforcement multi-agents hors ligne basé sur la théorie des jeux
fr.summary: Étend les Oracles de réponse d'espace politique pour la résolution de jeux hors ligne en quantifiant l'incertitude dans les ensembles de données limités et en sélectionnant de manière conservatrice les stratégies avec une probabilité plus élevée de regret faible. Introduit COffeE-PSRO, qui combine les principes de conservatisme avec un solveur de métastratégie novateur conçu pour les paramètres où la vérification du véritable équilibre est impossible.

de.title: Konservative Gleichgewichtsfindung in offline spieltheoretischem Multi-Agenten-Verstärkungslernens
de.summary: Erweitert Policy Space Response Oracles für offline Spiellösung durch Quantifizierung von Unsicherheit in begrenzten Datensätzen und konservative Auswahl von Strategien mit höherer Wahrscheinlichkeit geringen Bedauerns. Führt COffeE-PSRO ein, das Konservatismusprinzipien mit einem neuartigen Meta-Strategie-Löser kombiniert, der für Szenarien konzipiert ist, in denen die Verifikation eines wahren Gleichgewichts nicht durchführbar ist.

es.title: Descubrimiento de equilibrio conservador en aprendizaje por refuerzo multi-agente offline basado en teoría de juegos
es.summary: Extiende los Oráculos de Espacio de Política de Respuesta para la resolución de juegos offline cuantificando la incertidumbre en conjuntos de datos limitados y seleccionando conservadoramente estrategias con mayor probabilidad de bajo arrepentimiento. Introduce COffeE-PSRO, que combina principios de conservadurismo con un novedoso solucionador de metaestrategia diseñado para configuraciones donde verificar el verdadero equilibrio es inviable.

title: NeuroHex: Highly-Efficient Hex Coordinate System for Creating World Models to Enable Adaptive AI
summary: Introduces NeuroHex, a hexagonal coordinate system inspired by grid cells in the human brain, providing 60° rotational symmetry and efficient geometric operations for world models in adaptive AI systems. The OSM2Hex conversion pipeline achieves 90-99% reduction in geometric complexity while preserving spatial structure for real-time spatial reasoning.
content:
arXiv:2603.00376v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: \textit{NeuroHex} is a hexagonal coordinate system designed to support highly efficient world models and reference frames for online adaptive AI systems. Inspired by the hexadirectional firing structure of grid cells in the human brain, NeuroHex adopts a cubic isometric hexagonal coordinate formulation that provides full 60{\deg} rotational symmetry and low-cost translation, rotation and distance computation. We develop a mathematical framework that incorporates ring indexing, quantized angular encoding, and a hierarchical library of foundational, simple, and complex geometric shape primitives. These constructs allow low-overhead point-in-shape tests and spatial matching operations that are expensive in Cartesian coordinate systems. To support realistic settings, the NeuroHex framework can process OpenStreetMap (OSM) data sets using an OSM-to-NeuroHex (\textit{OSM2Hex}) conversion tool. The OSM2Hex spatial abstraction processing pipeline can achieve a reduction of 90-99\% in geometric complexity while maintaining the relevant spatial structure map for navigation. Our initial results, based on actual city and neighborhood scale data sets, demonstrate that NeuroHex offers a highly efficient substrate for building dynamic world models to enable adaptive spatial reasoning in autonomous AI systems with continuous online learning capability.

zh.title: NeuroHex: 高效六边形坐标系统用于创建世界模型以实现自适应人工智能
zh.summary: 介绍NeuroHex，一个受人脑网格细胞启发的六边形坐标系统，提供60°旋转对称性和高效的几何运算，适用于自适应AI系统中的世界模型。OSM2Hex转换管道在保持空间结构的同时实现了90-99%的几何复杂性降低，用于实时空间推理。

fr.title: NeuroHex: Système de coordonnées hexagonales hautement efficace pour créer des modèles du monde pour l'IA adaptative
fr.summary: Introduit NeuroHex, un système de coordonnées hexagonal inspiré par les cellules de grille du cerveau humain, fournissant une symétrie de rotation de 60° et des opérations géométriques efficaces pour les modèles du monde dans les systèmes d'IA adaptative. Le pipeline de conversion OSM2Hex réalise une réduction de 90-99% de la complexité géométrique tout en préservant la structure spatiale pour le raisonnement spatial en temps réel.

de.title: NeuroHex: Hocheffizientes Sechskantkoordinatensystem zur Erstellung von Weltmodellen für adaptive KI
de.summary: Führt NeuroHex ein, ein von Gitterzellen im menschlichen Gehirn inspiriertes hexagonales Koordinatensystem, das 60°-Rotationssymmetrie und effiziente geometrische Operationen für Weltmodelle in adaptiven KI-Systemen bietet. Die OSM2Hex-Konvertierungspipeline erreicht eine 90-99%ige Reduktion der geometrischen Komplexität, während die räumliche Struktur für räumliches Echtzeit-Reasoning erhalten bleibt.

es.title: NeuroHex: Sistema de coordenadas hexagonal altamente eficiente para crear modelos del mundo para IA adaptativa
es.summary: Introduce NeuroHex, un sistema de coordenadas hexagonal inspirado en células de red del cerebro humano, proporcionando simetría rotacional de 60° y operaciones geométricas eficientes para modelos del mundo en sistemas de IA adaptativa. El pipeline de conversión OSM2Hex logra una reducción del 90-99% en la complejidad geométrica mientras preserva la estructura espacial para razonamiento espacial en tiempo real.

title: Confusion-Aware Rubric Optimization for LLM-based Automated Grading
summary: Proposes CARO (Confusion-Aware Rubric Optimization), which decomposes error signals using confusion matrices to address specific misclassification patterns individually rather than aggregating mixed errors. By synthesizing targeted fixing patches for dominant error modes, the framework prevents conflicting guidance and demonstrates significant improvements in automated grading accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2603.00451v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Accurate and unambiguous guidelines are critical for large language model (LLM) based graders, yet manually crafting these prompts is often sub-optimal as LLMs can misinterpret expert guidelines or lack necessary domain specificity. Consequently, the field has moved toward automated prompt optimization to refine grading guidelines without the burden of manual trial and error. However, existing frameworks typically aggregate independent and unstructured error samples into a single update step, resulting in "rule dilution" where conflicting constraints weaken the model's grading logic. To address these limitations, we introduce Confusion-Aware Rubric Optimization (CARO), a novel framework that enhances accuracy and computational efficiency by structurally separating error signals. CARO leverages the confusion matrix to decompose monolithic error signals into distinct modes, allowing for the diagnosis and repair of specific misclassification patterns individually. By synthesizing targeted "fixing patches" for dominant error modes and employing a diversity-aware selection mechanism, the framework prevents guidance conflict and eliminates the need for resource-heavy nested refinement loops. Empirical evaluations on teacher education and STEM datasets demonstrate that CARO significantly outperforms existing SOTA methods. These results suggest that replacing mixed-error aggregation with surgical, mode-specific repair yields robust improvements in automated assessment scalability and precision.

zh.title: 混淆感知评分标准优化用于基于LLM的自动评分
zh.summary: 提出CARO（混淆感知评分标准优化），通过使用混淆矩阵分解错误信号，单独解决特定的分类错误模式，而不是聚合混合错误。通过为主要错误模式综合有针对性的修复补丁，该框架防止冲突指导，并在自动评分准确性中展示显著的改进。

fr.title: Optimisation de rubrique consciente de la confusion pour la notation automatisée basée sur LLM
fr.summary: Propose CARO (Optimisation de rubrique consciente de la confusion), qui décompose les signaux d'erreur en utilisant des matrices de confusion pour aborder les modèles de classification erronée spécifiques individuellement plutôt que d'agréger les erreurs mixtes. En synthétisant des correctifs ciblés pour les modes d'erreur dominants, le cadre prévient les conseils conflictuels et démontre des améliorations significatives en matière de précision de la notation automatisée.

de.title: Verwirrungs-bewusste Rubrik-Optimierung für LLM-basierte automatisierte Bewertung
de.summary: Schlägt CARO (Verwirrungs-bewusste Rubrik-Optimierung) vor, das Fehlersignale mithilfe von Verwirrungs-matrizen dekompsoniert, um spezifische Fehlklassifizierungsmuster einzeln anzugehen, anstatt gemischte Fehler zu aggregieren. Durch die Synthese gezielter Fixierungsflicken für dominante Fehlermodi verhindert das Framework widersprüchliche Anleitung und zeigt signifikante Verbesserungen in der Genauigkeit der automatisierten Bewertung.

es.title: Optimización de rúbrica consciente de confusión para calificación automatizada basada en LLM
es.summary: Propone CARO (Optimización de rúbrica consciente de confusión), que descompone señales de error utilizando matrices de confusión para abordar patrones específicos de clasificación errónea individualmente en lugar de agregar errores mixtos. Al sintetizar parches de corrección específicos para modos de error dominantes, el marco previene orientación conflictiva y demuestra mejoras significativas en la precisión de la calificación automatizada.

title: MED-COPILOT: A Medical Assistant Powered by GraphRAG and Similar Patient Case Retrieval
summary: Presents MED-COPILOT, a clinical decision-support system combining GraphRAG retrieval of WHO/NICE guidelines with semantic-keyword retrieval of similar patient cases from 36,000 clinical records. The system addresses LLM hallucination and long-document integration challenges while maintaining transparent evidence inspection and showing consistent improvements over standard RAG approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.00460v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Clinical decision-making requires synthesizing heterogeneous evidence, including patient histories, clinical guidelines, and trajectories of comparable cases. While large language models (LLMs) offer strong reasoning capabilities, they remain prone to hallucinations and struggle to integrate long, structured medical documents. We present MED-COPILOT, an interactive clinical decision-support system designed for clinicians and medical trainees, which combines guideline-grounded GraphRAG retrieval with hybrid semantic-keyword similar-patient retrieval to support transparent and evidence-aware clinical reasoning. The system builds a structured knowledge graph from WHO and NICE guidelines, applies community-level summarization for efficient retrieval, and maintains a 36,000-case similar-patient database derived from SOAP-normalized MIMIC-IV notes and Synthea-generated records.
  We evaluate our framework on clinical note completion and medical question answering, and demonstrate that it consistently outperforms parametric LLM baselines and standard RAG, improving both generation fidelity and clinical reasoning accuracy. The full system is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/Cryo3978/Med_GraphRAG , enabling users to inspect retrieved evidence, visualize token-level similarity contributions, and conduct guided follow-up analysis. Our results demonstrate a practical and interpretable approach to integrating structured guideline knowledge with patient-level analogical evidence for clinical LLMs.

zh.title: MED-COPILOT：由GraphRAG和相似患者病例检索驱动的医学助手
zh.summary: 介绍MED-COPILOT，一个临床决策支持系统，将GraphRAG检索WHO/NICE指南与来自36,000份临床记录的相似患者病例的语义-关键词检索相结合。该系统解决了LLM幻觉和长文档集成挑战，同时保持透明的证据检查，并显示出相对于标准RAG方法的持续改进。

fr.title: MED-COPILOT : Un assistant médical alimenté par GraphRAG et la récupération de cas de patients similaires
fr.summary: Présente MED-COPILOT, un système d'aide à la décision clinique combinant la récupération GraphRAG des directives de l'OMS/NICE avec la récupération sémantique-mots clés de cas de patients similaires à partir de 36 000 dossiers cliniques. Le système résout les défis d'hallucination des LLM et d'intégration de documents longs tout en maintenant une inspection des preuves transparente et montrant des améliorations constantes par rapport aux approches RAG standard.

de.title: MED-COPILOT: Ein medizinischer Assistent, der von GraphRAG und dem Abrufen ähnlicher Patientenfälle unterstützt wird
de.summary: Stellt MED-COPILOT vor, ein klinisches Entscheidungsunterstützungssystem, das GraphRAG-Abruf von WHO/NICE-Richtlinien mit semantisch-schlüsselwort-gestütztem Abruf ähnlicher Patientenfälle aus 36.000 klinischen Aufzeichnungen kombiniert. Das System behebt LLM-Halluzinationen und Herausforderungen bei der Dokumentenintegration, während es eine transparente Beweisbewertung beibehält und konsistente Verbesserungen gegenüber Standard-RAG-Ansätzen zeigt.

es.title: MED-COPILOT: Un asistente médico impulsado por GraphRAG y recuperación de casos de pacientes similares
es.summary: Presenta MED-COPILOT, un sistema de apoyo a la decisión clínica que combina la recuperación de GraphRAG de directrices de la OMS/NICE con la recuperación semántica-palabra clave de casos de pacientes similares de 36.000 registros clínicos. El sistema aborda los desafíos de alucinación de LLM e integración de documentos largos mientras mantiene una inspección de pruebas transparente y muestra mejoras consistentes sobre los enfoques RAG estándar.

title: Optimizing In-Context Demonstrations for LLM-based Automated Grading
summary: Introduces GUIDE (Grading Using Iteratively Designed Exemplars), which reframes automated grading as a boundary-focused optimization problem by identifying semantically similar examples with different grades. The framework generates discriminative rationales explaining precise rubric decision boundaries, showing exceptional gains on borderline cases and improved rubric adherence.
content:
arXiv:2603.00465v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automated assessment of open-ended student responses is a critical capability for scaling personalized feedback in education. While large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in grading tasks via in-context learning (ICL), their reliability is heavily dependent on the selection of few-shot exemplars and the construction of high-quality rationales. Standard retrieval methods typically select examples based on semantic similarity, which often fails to capture subtle decision boundaries required for rubric adherence. Furthermore, manually crafting the expert rationales needed to guide these models can be a significant bottleneck. To address these limitations, we introduce GUIDE (Grading Using Iteratively Designed Exemplars), a framework that reframes exemplar selection and refinement in automated grading as a boundary-focused optimization problem. GUIDE operates on a continuous loop of selection and refinement, employing novel contrastive operators to identify "boundary pairs" that are semantically similar but possess different grades. We enhance exemplars by generating discriminative rationales that explicitly articulate why a response receives a specific score to the exclusion of adjacent grades. Extensive experiments across datasets in physics, chemistry, and pedagogical content knowledge demonstrate that GUIDE significantly outperforms standard retrieval baselines. By focusing the model's attention on the precise edges of rubric, our approach shows exceptionally robust gains on borderline cases and improved rubric adherence. GUIDE paves the way for trusted, scalable assessment systems that align closely with human pedagogical standards.

zh.title: 为基于LLM的自动评分优化上下文演示
zh.summary: 介绍GUIDE（使用迭代设计的示例进行评分），它通过识别具有不同等级的语义相似示例，将自动评分重新定义为边界聚焦的优化问题。该框架生成区分性理由，解释精确的评分标准决策边界，在边界情况下显示出异常收益，并改进了评分标准的遵守情况。

fr.title: Optimisation des démonstrations en contexte pour l'évaluation automatisée basée sur le LLM
fr.summary: Introduit GUIDE (Grading Using Iteratively Designed Exemplars), qui redéfinit l'évaluation automatisée comme un problème d'optimisation axé sur les frontières en identifiant des exemples sémantiquement similaires avec des notes différentes. Le cadre génère des justifications discriminantes expliquant les limites précises des décisions de rubrique, montrant des gains exceptionnels sur les cas limites et une meilleure conformité à la rubrique.

de.title: Optimierung von In-Context-Demonstrationen für LLM-basierte automatisierte Bewertung
de.summary: Führt GUIDE (Grading Using Iteratively Designed Exemplars) ein, das die automatisierte Bewertung als Grenzwert-fokussiertes Optimierungsproblem umgestaltet, indem semantisch ähnliche Beispiele mit unterschiedlichen Noten identifiziert werden. Das Framework generiert diskriminative Begründungen, die präzise Rubriken-Entscheidungsgrenzen erklären, und zeigt außergewöhnliche Gewinne bei Grenzfällen und verbesserte Rubrik-Einhaltung.

es.title: Optimización de demostraciones en contexto para calificación automática basada en LLM
es.summary: Presenta GUIDE (Calificación usando ejemplares diseñados iterativamente), que reformula la calificación automática como un problema de optimización centrado en límites identificando ejemplos semánticamente similares con diferentes calificaciones. El marco genera justificaciones discriminativas que explican límites precisos de decisiones de rúbrica, mostrando ganancias excepcionales en casos fronterizos y mejor adherencia a la rúbrica.

title: Why Not? Solver-Grounded Certificates for Explainable Mission Planning
summary: Proposes a faithfulness-first approach to explainability in satellite scheduling by deriving explanations directly from optimization models rather than post-hoc reasoning layers. Certificates include minimal infeasible subsets for rejections and inverse solves for what-if queries, achieving perfect soundness and counterfactual validity compared to post-hoc baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.00469v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Operators of Earth observation satellites need justifications for scheduling decisions: why a request was selected, rejected, or what changes would make it schedulable. Existing approaches construct post-hoc reasoning layers independent of the optimizer, risking non-causal attributions, incomplete constraint conjunctions, and solver-path dependence. We take a faithfulness-first approach: every explanation is a certificate derived from the optimization model itself: minimal infeasible subsets for rejections, tight constraints and contrastive trade-offs for selections, and inverse solves for what-if queries. On a scheduling instance with structurally distinct constraint interactions, certificates achieve perfect soundness with respect to the solver's constraint model (15/15 cited-constraint checks), counterfactual validity (7/7), and stability (Jaccard = 1.0 across 28 seed-pairs), while a post-hoc baseline produces non-causal attributions in 29% of cases and misses constraint conjunctions in every multi-cause rejection. A scalability analysis up to 200 orders and 30 satellites confirms practical extraction times for operational batches.

zh.title: 为什么不行？用于可解释任务规划的求解器基础证书
zh.summary: 提出了一个以真实性为首要的方法来解决卫星调度的可解释性问题，通过直接从优化模型而不是事后推理层中提取解释。证书包括拒绝的最小不可行子集和用于假设查询的逆求解，与事后基线相比实现了完美的合理性和反事实有效性。

fr.title: Pourquoi pas ? Certificats fondés sur le solveur pour une planification de mission explicable
fr.summary: Propose une approche basée sur la fidélité d'abord pour l'explicabilité dans l'ordonnancement des satellites en dérivant les explications directement à partir des modèles d'optimisation plutôt que des couches de raisonnement post-hoc. Les certificats incluent des sous-ensembles infaisables minimaux pour les rejets et des résolutions inverses pour les requêtes contrefactuelles, obtenant une solidité parfaite et une validité contrefactuelle par rapport aux bases post-hoc.

de.title: Warum nicht? Solver-gestützte Zertifikate für erklärbare Missionsplanung
de.summary: Schlägt einen treue-ersten Ansatz zur Erklärbarkeit in der Satellitenplanung vor, indem Erklärungen direkt aus Optimierungsmodellen und nicht aus Nachbegriff-Begründungsebenen abgeleitet werden. Zertifikate umfassen minimale nicht durchführbare Teilmengen für Ablehnungen und inverse Lösungen für Was-wäre-wenn-Abfragen und erreichen perfekte Solidität und kontrafaktische Validität im Vergleich zu Nachbegriff-Grundlinien.

es.title: ¿Por qué no? Certificados basados en solucionadores para planificación de misión explicable
es.summary: Propone un enfoque primero la fidelidad para la explicabilidad en la programación de satélites derivando explicaciones directamente de modelos de optimización en lugar de capas de razonamiento post-hoc. Los certificados incluyen subconjuntos infactibles mínimos para rechazos e inversas para consultas contrafácticas, logrando solidez perfecta y validez contrafáctica en comparación con líneas base post-hoc.

title: From Goals to Aspects, Revisited: An NFR Pattern Language for Agentic AI Systems
summary: Extends the goals-to-aspects methodology to agentic AI systems, presenting 12 reusable patterns addressing crosscutting concerns like security, reliability, observability, and cost management. Introduces four agent-specific patterns for tool-scope sandboxing, prompt injection detection, token budget management, and action audit trails to systematically engineer reliable production agentic systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.00472v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic AI systems exhibit numerous crosscutting concerns -- security, observability, cost management, fault tolerance -- that are poorly modularized in current implementations, contributing to the high failure rate of AI projects in reaching production. The goals-to-aspects methodology proposed at RE 2004 demonstrated that aspects can be systematically discovered from i* goal models by identifying non-functional soft-goals that crosscut functional goals. This paper revisits and extends that methodology to the agentic AI domain. We present a pattern language of 12 reusable patterns organized across four NFR categories (security, reliability, observability, cost management), each mapping an i* goal model to a concrete aspect implementation using an AOP framework for Rust. Four patterns address agent-specific crosscutting concerns absent from traditional AOP literature: tool-scope sandboxing, prompt injection detection, token budget management, and action audit trails. We extend the V-graph model to capture how agent tasks simultaneously contribute to functional goals and non-functional soft-goals. We validate the pattern language through a case study analyzing an open-source autonomous agent framework, demonstrating how goal-driven aspect discovery systematically identifies and modularizes crosscutting concerns. The pattern language offers a principled approach for engineering reliable agentic AI systems through early identification of crosscutting concerns.

zh.title: 从目标到方面，重新审视：代理型AI系统的NFR模式语言
zh.summary: 将目标到方面的方法论扩展到代理型AI系统，提出12个可复用模式，解决安全、可靠性、可观察性和成本管理等交叉问题。引入四个特定于代理的模式，用于工具范围沙箱、提示注入检测、令牌预算管理和操作审计跟踪，以系统地工程化可靠的生产代理系统。

fr.title: Des objectifs aux aspects, revisités : Un langage de modèles NFR pour les systèmes d'IA agentive
fr.summary: Étend la méthodologie des objectifs aux aspects vers les systèmes d'IA agentive, présentant 12 modèles réutilisables traitant des préoccupations transversales comme la sécurité, la fiabilité, l'observabilité et la gestion des coûts. Introduit quatre modèles spécifiques aux agents pour le bac à sable de portée d'outil, la détection d'injection de requête, la gestion du budget de jetons et les pistes d'audit d'action.

de.title: Von Zielen zu Aspekten, überarbeitet: Eine NFR-Mustersprache für agentengesteuerte KI-Systeme
de.summary: Erweitert die Ziele-zu-Aspekte-Methodik auf agentengesteuerte KI-Systeme und stellt 12 wiederverwendbare Muster vor, die sich mit übergreifenden Belangen wie Sicherheit, Zuverlässigkeit, Beobachtbarkeit und Kostenverwaltung befassen. Führt vier agentsspezifische Muster für Tool-Scope-Sandboxing, Prompt-Injection-Erkennung, Token-Budget-Verwaltung und Action-Audit-Trails ein.

es.title: De objetivos a aspectos, revisitados: Un lenguaje de patrones NFR para sistemas de IA agentiva
es.summary: Extiende la metodología de objetivos a aspectos a sistemas de IA agentiva, presentando 12 patrones reutilizables que abordan preocupaciones transversales como seguridad, confiabilidad, observabilidad y gestión de costos. Introduce cuatro patrones específicos de agentes para aislamiento de ámbito de herramienta, detección de inyección de solicitudes y rastros de auditoría de acciones.

title: LifeEval: A Multimodal Benchmark for Assistive AI in Egocentric Daily Life Tasks
summary: Introduces LifeEval, a multimodal benchmark with 4,075 QA pairs evaluating real-time, task-oriented human-AI collaboration from an egocentric perspective covering holistic task evaluation, first-person perception, and collaborative interaction through natural dialogue. Evaluation of 26 state-of-the-art models reveals substantial challenges in achieving timely, effective, and adaptive real-world assistance.
content:
arXiv:2603.00490v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The rapid progress of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) marks a significant step toward artificial general intelligence, offering great potential for augmenting human capabilities. However, their ability to provide effective assistance in dynamic, real-world environments remains largely underexplored. Existing video benchmarks predominantly assess passive understanding through retrospective analysis or isolated perception tasks, failing to capture the interactive and adaptive nature of real-time user assistance. To bridge this gap, we introduce LifeEval, a multimodal benchmark designed to evaluate real-time, task-oriented human-AI collaboration in daily life from an egocentric perspective. LifeEval emphasizes three key aspects: task-oriented holistic evaluation, egocentric real-time perception from continuous first-person streams, and human-assistant collaborative interaction through natural dialogues. Constructed via a rigorous annotation pipeline, the benchmark comprises 4,075 high-quality question-answer pairs across 6 core capability dimensions. Extensive evaluations of 26 state-of-the-art MLLMs on LifeEval reveal substantial challenges in achieving timely, effective and adaptive interaction, highlighting essential directions for advancing human-centered interactive intelligence.

zh.title: LifeEval：以自我中心视角进行日常生活任务辅助AI的多模态基准
zh.summary: 介绍LifeEval，一个包含4,075个QA对的多模态基准，从自我中心视角评估实时、面向任务的人工智能协作，涵盖整体任务评估、第一人称感知和通过自然对话的协作交互。对26个最先进模型的评估揭示了在实现及时、有效和自适应实际辅助方面的重大挑战。

fr.title: LifeEval : Un benchmark multimodal pour l'IA assistive dans les tâches de la vie quotidienne égocentrique
fr.summary: Introduit LifeEval, un benchmark multimodal avec 4 075 paires QA évaluant la collaboration homme-IA en temps réel et orientée vers les tâches d'une perspective égocentrique couvrant l'évaluation holistique des tâches, la perception en première personne et l'interaction collaborative par le dialogue naturel. L'évaluation de 26 modèles de pointe révèle des défis substantiels dans la réalisation d'une assistance timée, efficace et adaptative.

de.title: LifeEval: Ein multimodales Benchmark für unterstützende KI bei alltäglichen Aufgaben aus egozentrischem Blickwinkel
de.summary: Stellt LifeEval vor, ein multimodales Benchmark mit 4.075 QA-Paaren, das echtzeitgesteuerte, aufgabenorientierte Mensch-KI-Zusammenarbeit aus einer egozentrischen Perspektive bewertet und ganzheitliche Aufgabenbewertung, Wahrnehmung aus der ersten Person und kollaborative Interaktion durch natürliche Dialoge abdeckt. Die Bewertung von 26 hochmodernen Modellen zeigt erhebliche Herausforderungen bei der Bereitstellung zeitnaher, wirksamer und adaptiver realer Unterstützung.

es.title: LifeEval: Un benchmark multimodal para IA asistida en tareas cotidianas desde una perspectiva egocéntrica
es.summary: Introduce LifeEval, un benchmark multimodal con 4.075 pares de preguntas y respuestas que evalúa la colaboración humano-IA en tiempo real orientada a tareas desde una perspectiva egocéntrica cubriendo evaluación de tareas holística, percepción en primera persona e interacción colaborativa a través del diálogo natural. La evaluación de 26 modelos de última generación revela desafíos sustanciales en lograr asistencia oportuna, efectiva y adaptativa en el mundo real.

title: AI Runtime Infrastructure
summary: Introduces a distinct execution-time layer operating above models and below applications that actively observes, reasons over, and intervenes in agent behavior to optimize task success, latency, token efficiency, reliability, and safety. Unlike passive monitoring systems, runtime infrastructure treats execution as an optimization surface enabling adaptive memory management and failure recovery over long-horizon workflows.
content:
arXiv:2603.00495v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce AI Runtime Infrastructure, a distinct execution-time layer that operates above the model and below the application, actively observing, reasoning over, and intervening in agent behavior to optimize task success, latency, token efficiency, reliability, and safety while the agent is running. Unlike model-level optimizations or passive logging systems, runtime infrastructure treats execution itself as an optimization surface, enabling adaptive memory management, failure detection, recovery, and policy enforcement over long-horizon agent workflows.

zh.title: AI运行时基础设施
zh.summary: 介绍一个在模型之上、应用之下运行的执行时层，主动观察、推理和干预代理行为，以优化任务成功、延迟、令牌效率、可靠性和安全性。与被动监控系统不同，运行时基础设施将执行视为优化表面，在长期工作流中实现自适应内存管理和故障恢复。

fr.title: Infrastructure d'exécution pour l'IA
fr.summary: Introduit une couche d'exécution distincte fonctionnant au-dessus des modèles et en dessous des applications qui observe activement, raisonne sur et intervient dans le comportement des agents pour optimiser la réussite des tâches, la latence, l'efficacité des jetons, la fiabilité et la sécurité. Contrairement aux systèmes de surveillance passifs, l'infrastructure d'exécution traite l'exécution comme une surface d'optimisation permettant la gestion de la mémoire adaptative et la récupération après défaillance sur les flux de travail à long terme.

de.title: KI-Laufzeitinfrastruktur
de.summary: Führt eine separate Ausführungsschicht ein, die über Modellen und unter Anwendungen arbeitet und das Verhalten von Agenten aktiv beobachtet, darüber nachdenkt und eingreift, um Aufgabenerfolg, Latenz, Token-Effizienz, Zuverlässigkeit und Sicherheit zu optimieren. Im Gegensatz zu passiven Überwachungssystemen behandelt die Laufzeitinfrastruktur die Ausführung als Optimierungsfläche, die adaptive Speicherverwaltung und Fehlerwiederherstellung über langfristige Workflows ermöglicht.

es.title: Infraestructura de tiempo de ejecución de IA
es.summary: Introduce una capa de tiempo de ejecución distinta que opera sobre modelos y debajo de aplicaciones que observa activamente, razona e interviene en el comportamiento del agente para optimizar el éxito de la tarea, latencia, eficiencia de tokens, confiabilidad y seguridad. A diferencia de los sistemas de monitoreo pasivos, la infraestructura de tiempo de ejecución trata la ejecución como una superficie de optimización que permite la gestión de memoria adaptativa y la recuperación de fallos en flujos de trabajo de largo horizonte.
